title: US Forces Japan Supports Balikatan 2026 Operations While Monitoring PLA Regional Activity
summary: US Forces Japan coordinates with Japanese Self-Defense Forces on Balikatan 2026 support and maintains readiness posture amid increased PLA activity across the Indo-Pacific.
contents:
## USFJ Regional Operations and Readiness

### Balikatan Support
US Forces Japan continues coordination with Japanese Self-Defense Forces in support of Balikatan 2026 operations. Marine Corps units from III Marine Expeditionary Force contribute to the exercises alongside JGSDF Amphibious Rapid Deployment Brigade elements, practicing joint island defense and sea denial scenarios.

### PLA Activity Monitoring
USFJ maintains elevated monitoring of PLA activity across the region, with the PLAN carrier Liaoning's transit through the Taiwan Strait and subsequent South China Sea operations drawing particular attention. Forward-deployed US forces at Kadena Air Base, Yokota Air Base, Misawa Air Base, and Marine Corps installations across Okinawa provide continuous surveillance capabilities.

### Regional Force Posture
The three-carrier presence in the Middle East — Abraham Lincoln, Gerald R. Ford, and George H.W. Bush — has concentrated significant US naval power away from the Indo-Pacific theater. This redeployment heightens the importance of USFJ's bilateral cooperation with Japanese forces and the expanded multilateral framework demonstrated through Balikatan participation.

### Alliance Readiness
USFJ Commander has emphasized the ironclad nature of the US-Japan alliance despite the Middle East operational demands. Joint interoperability training continues at a high tempo, with Japanese forces assuming greater responsibility for regional deterrence consistent with Tokyo's expanded defense posture. The deployment of JGSDF anti-ship missiles during Balikatan underscores Japan's growing capability contribution to allied defense.
